nodes.context: Powerful 'Ndrangheta clan based in Gioia Tauro, Calabria, involved in drug trafficking, public works, and port control.
Allied clan of the Piromalli, often referred to as the military arm, based in Gioia Tauro.
Rival clan involved in a bloody feud with the Piromalli-Molè clan in the 1950s.
'Ndrangheta clan from San Luca, rotated the position of capo crimine with the Piromalli family since the mid-1970s.
Powerful 'Ndrangheta clan from Reggio Calabria, rival of Piromalli during the First 'Ndrangheta War.
'Ndrangheta clan involved in cocaine trafficking through Gioia Tauro port, allied with Piromalli.
Powerful 'Ndrangheta clan involved in agro-industry in the Gioia Tauro plain.
'Ndrangheta clan that previously controlled the Milan fruit and vegetable market, later taken over by Piromalli.
Powerful 'Ndrangheta boss from Siderno, part of a triumvirate with Piromalli and Tripodo until the First 'Ndrangheta War.
Boss of Reggio Calabria, part of a triumvirate with Piromalli and Macrì until the First 'Ndrangheta War.

edges.context: Allied through family ties, often referred to as Piromalli-Molè clan; Molè served as military arm.
Bloody feud in the 1950s, resulting in the killing of Antonino Piromalli in 1956.
Rotated the position of capo crimine among themselves since the mid-1970s.
Conflict over business interests contributed to the First 'Ndrangheta War (1974-1977); Piromalli killed Giorgio De Stefano.
Both control the Gioia Tauro port area and cooperate in cocaine trafficking, with Pesce transporting cocaine in exchange for a percentage.
Both heavily involved in Calabria's agro-industry, exploiting EU subsidies and controlling land.
Piromalli took over control of the Milan fruit and vegetable market from the Morabito 'ndrina.
Part of a triumvirate with Domenico Tripodo from the 1960s until the First 'Ndrangheta War.
Part of a triumvirate with Antonio Macrì from the 1960s until the First 'Ndrangheta War.
Both were part of the triumvirate with Piromalli until the First 'Ndrangheta War.
